mnemonics.title: Service Models Remembered
mnemonics.content:
IaaS: Infrastructure = Server + Storage + Networking
PaaS: Platform = IaaS + Runtime + Middleware + Development Tools
SaaS: Software = IaaS + PaaS + Application

Mnemonic: 'I P S' - Imagine, Provide, Software (Think 'I'm Providing Software' for SaaS)

mnemonics.title: NIST 5 Characteristics
mnemonics.content:
On-demand Self-service
Broad network access
Resource pooling
Rapid elasticity
Measured Service

Mnemonic: 'OBERM' - One Broad River Enters Many Streams (or 'OBERMS')

acronyms.usage: Virtualized computing resources over internet (AWS EC2, Azure VMs)
Development/deployment platform (Google App Engine, Azure App Service)
Software delivered over internet (Gmail, Salesforce, Microsoft 365)

acronyms.expansion: National Institute of Standards and Technology
acronyms.usage: US agency that defines cloud computing standards and characteristics
Software emulation of physical computer running on shared hardware
Set of protocols enabling different software applications to communicate
Contract defining expected service standards between provider and customer

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted pyramid: Top (smallest) = SaaS, Middle = PaaS, Bottom (largest) = IaaS. As you go down, provider manages less, customer manages more.
Use Venn diagram: Left circle = Private, Right circle = Public, Overlap = Hybrid. Community = Separate circle intersecting all three.
5 fingers on one hand - each finger represents one NIST characteristic. Associate each with real-life examples.

analogies.analogy: Like electricity from the grid - you don't build your own power plant, you just plug in and pay for what you use.
Renting a empty warehouse - you bring your own goods, manage everything inside, landlord maintains building.
Renting a fully equipped restaurant kitchen - infrastructure provided, you bring recipes and cook.
Going to a restaurant - everything is prepared for you, you just eat and pay for the meal.
An apartment building - multiple families live separately (their data isolated) but share common infrastructure (elevator, parking).

analogies.anal analogy: Elastic band stretching and shrinking - cloud resources expand and contract based on workload demand.

analogies.analogy: A public library - books (resources) are shared among many users, dynamically assigned as needed.
